artPrompt: A charged moment of suspended power: an upraised hand on the town green hovers over a borrowed townsperson, golden afternoon washes pooling around them, while at the lower edge a small green-and-white voter sprite pops into being, crumbs scattering like flecked ink. Loose washes suggest folding tables and casseroles behind, picket fences dissolving into negative space. Wholesome civic fervor caught mid-gesture, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Effective Until Your Next Snack
text:
Gain control of target creature the frontrunner controls until you next eat a snack. The moment you eat any snack — bite, lick, or principled nibble — control reverts and you create a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token, satisfied and slightly crumby.
If you never snack again, you have bigger problems than this card, and you keep the creature, and the frog is concerned for you.
flavorText: Every ordinance in this town has a snack-based sunset clause. It's the only thing everyone agrees on.

extras.timingGag: until you next eat a snack
